channel.title: Home
channel.description: The Trust-In Megastudy is a collaborative research project to strengthen trust in climate scientists in the United States.

item.title: 📌 Preregistered & launching data collection!
item.dc:creator: Jan Pfänder
item.description:
🎯 A big milestone for the Strengthening Trust in Climate Scientists Megastudy: our preregistration is now public, and data collection is officially underway!

📌 The preregistration locks in our study design, hypotheses, sampling plan, and analysis strategy before we see any results — a key step for transparent, credible science.

🔗 You can read the full preregistration on our website. It is registered on Zenodo: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20160211

🧪 We’re testing 20 short, text-based interventions (plus a control) designed to strengthen trust in US climate scientists, with a target sample of 22,000 participants recruited via CloudResearch.

A huge thank you again to all our collaborators whose interventions made it into the final set — it’s exciting to finally see this go live. Stay tuned for further updates! ✨

🔗 More information

For more information about the project and the team, visit our website.

💌 Questions? Email us anytime at trustclimsci.megastudy@gmail.com

item.title: ⌛ Time’s up! Thank you all for your great submissions! 🎉
item.dc:creator: Jan Pfänder
item.description:
🌍🔬 Thank you all for submitting your fantastic intervention proposals to our Strengthening Trust in Climate Scientists Megastudy!

💪 You’ve exceeded even our most optimistic expectations — we received 1051 intervention proposals from 80 different research teams2 (25 teams submitted two interventions), from 65 different institutions.

💚 We’re especially proud that so many submissions came from early-career researchers who want to strengthen trust in science.

🕵 Our advisory board will now begin reviewing all interventions. The process is fully anonymized — only the research lead (Jan) can link submissions to their authors, and cannot participate in the voting.

🗓 We expect the review and selection process to be completed by mid-December. Stay tuned for updates!️

To end with a little fun fact: Since we received a couple of apologies for late submissions — don’t feel bad, you’re not alone, it really happens to (almost) all of us (Figure 1) 😅:

Once again, a huge thank you to everyone who contributed to this collaborative effort. Your expertise, creativity, and collaborative spirit make this project possible. 💫

🔗 More information

For more information about the project and the team, visit our website.

💌 Questions? Email us anytime at
trustclimsci.megastudy@gmail.com

Footnotes

  1. Initially, we’ve received 107 proposals, but two got retracted from the respective authors shortly after the submission deadline.↩︎

  2. some “teams” are actually individual researchers—24 of the 105 submissions are single-authored↩︎

item.title: Now launched: Call for Collaboration
item.dc:creator: Jan Pfänder
item.description:
🤝 Collaborate with us!

The Strengthening Trust in Climate Scientists Megastudy is a collaborative research project to be run in the United States.

We invite researchers at any career stage—from students to principal investigators—as well as practitioners to submit intervention ideas.

Successful contributors will receive co-authorship in the primary publication arising from this project.

Interventions can be submitted by individuals or teams, with a maximum of two individuals per team. Each team may propose up to two interventions, which will be evaluated independently.

🔍 What We’re Looking For

We are seeking short, text-based informational interventions that could increase trust in climate scientists.
The most promising interventions will be selected and tested in a megastudy—a large-scale online experiment designed for robust, replicable results.

👉 Learn about submission rules and how to contribute on our Call for Collaborations page.

📝 Submit your intervention proposal via our designated submission survey.

🔗 More information

For more information about the project and the team, visit our website.

💌 Questions? Email us anytime at
trustclimsci.megastudy@gmail.com
